data_basis: PRODUCTION — energy prices; MISSING — all AS prices (DA/RT), DA-RT P(DA>RT) probability

data_sources.energy_prices: Smartbidder DA Energy forecast run 2026-05-24T06:56 CDT (production, 24 hourly values present)
data_sources.da_rt_forecast: NOT AVAILABLE — DA-RT Forecast CSV contains only Date column, no price columns
data_sources.da_as_prices: NOT AVAILABLE — DA Ancillary Prices CSV all null
data_sources.rt_as_prices: NOT AVAILABLE — RT Ancillary Prices CSV all null
data_sources.as_playbook: NOT AVAILABLE — recommend_as_position.py not run; 2026-05-25.json not present
data_sources.market_view: market-analyst 2026-05-25.md (Yes Energy BIDCLOSE + Enverus STPF + AG2 WSI)

assumptions.starting_soc_basis: Prior day (2026-05-24) schedule ended at SoC=0 MWh (HE22 full discharge). No Tenaska PTP HSL or actuals available to confirm. Worst-case = 0 assumed.
assumptions.hsl_source: ASSUMED — Tenaska PTP HSL fetch not executed; operations to confirm before 10:00 CT
assumptions.as_prices_basis: ESTIMATED from ERCOT seasonal pattern (May shoulder, high solar/ramp day) — no Smartbidder AS data received. Low confidence. AS revenue figures are indicative only.

strategy: Stack A — Solar Trough Charge + Dual-Hour Peak Discharge. Charge exclusively during solar price trough HE08-10 ($16.87-17.82 DA, cheapest of day) to 185 MWh SoC. No overnight charge — solar trough is $8-11 cheaper than overnight. Discharge 85 MW at HE19 ($49.52) then full 100 MW at HE20 ($59.67), peak-of-day. Offer Non-Spin + ECRS DA during idle hold HE11-17. Stop AS at HE18 to preserve full capacity for peak.
spread_convention: spread = DA - RT. Positive = DA expensive. HE19-20 spread direction uncertain (no P(DA>RT) data). DA discharge recommended on energy price dominance alone.

HE01.notes: SoC=0 MWh. Cannot discharge. Cannot offer discharge-based AS (RRS/ECRS/RegUp/Non-Spin). RegDown possible but AS price unknown and overnight value marginal. Idle.

HE02.notes: SoC=0. Overnight price $27.71 — $10.84 above solar trough HE09. No overnight charge; preserving economic advantage of HE08-10 solar charge.

HE03.notes: SoC=0. $27.03 vs solar trough $16.87 — $10.16 premium. Idle.

HE04.notes: SoC=0. Cheapest overnight hour ($26.19) but still $9.32 above HE09. Idle.

HE07.notes: SoC=0. Solar rising from HE07. Price $24.57 still above solar trough. Idle.

HE08.notes: PRIMARY CHARGE HOUR 1. $17.82 DA. Solar 7,521 MW COPHSL rising rapidly. SoC 0 -> 85 MWh (100 MW x 0.85 RTE). DA charge is safest — avoids RT congestion risk at GKS (West export MEDIUM binding probability; DA energy at node not affected by RT congestion).

HE09.notes: PRIMARY CHARGE HOUR 2. $16.87 = LOWEST DA PRICE of day. Solar 19,891 MW. Net load 20,931 MW (Yes) / 24,855 MW (Enverus) — daily trough. West export constraint MEDIUM binding. Congestion caution: RT at GKS may be below DA if WEST_TO_NORTH binds. DA charge cost is locked in at $16.87 regardless of RT congestion — DA bid eliminates nodal RT risk on charge side. SoC 85 -> 170 MWh.

HE10.notes: TOP-OFF charge. 18 MW partial charge: 18 x 0.85 = 15.3 MWh -> SoC 170 -> 185.3 MWh. Target 185 MWh vs 200 MWh max: reserve 15 MWh headroom for RegDown / RT absorption option. West congestion MEDIUM — same DA charge protection applies. Note: SoC rounded to 185 MWh in subsequent hours.

HE11.notes: Charge complete. SoC 185 MWh. Begin AS offering. Non-Spin 50 MW + ECRS 30 MW (total 80 MW AS, 20 MW margin). AS prices estimated — no Smartbidder data. West congestion may extend through HE11-13. Non-Spin DA AS price is hub-based, immune to nodal congestion, so safe to offer.

HE12.notes: Solar at 28,503 MW (Yes). Net load still near trough. AS offering continues. No energy action — DA energy at $21.64 far below discharge target.

HE13.notes: Solar peak 29,211 MW (Yes, HE13). Load rising (Monday ramp). West export pressure easing as net load rises. ECRS value ticking up slightly with net load ramp initiating.

HE14.notes: Solar declining. Load 71,500 MW range. Net load rising. AS offering: Non-Spin + ECRS.

HE15.notes: Evening ramp building. Cap out 17,309 MW. ECRS value rising as reserve margin tightens into afternoon.

HE16.notes: Load peak approaching HE17. Net load ramp accelerating. ECRS value elevated. Cap out at 17,311 MW (HE15-21). AS value stepping up.

HE17.notes: ERCOT load peak HE17 at 72,614 MW. Solar still 27,203 MW but declining. Net load 35,412 MW rising fast. ECRS and Non-Spin at their highest off-peak values as reserve margin tightens.

HE18.notes: STOP ALL AS. Preserve 185 MWh SoC for HE19-20 discharge. $31.51 DA energy too low to discharge here; save for $49-60 window. Net load 36,330 MW — ramp still early. Houston import constraint HIGH probability starting to build.

HE19.notes: DISCHARGE HOUR 1. 85 MW DA energy at $49.52. SoC 185 -> 100 MWh. Net load 39,817 MW (Yes) — ramp accelerating. Solar collapsing (17,783 MW). Houston import constraint HIGH probability. DA discharge locks in $49.52; DA-RT spread direction uncertain (no P(DA>RT) data) but DA revenue secured. Note: 15 MW capacity available for Non-Spin if AS price warrants but energy priority given price level.

HE20.notes: PRIMARY DISCHARGE HOUR. $59.67 DA = highest price of day. 100 MW full capacity. SoC 100 -> 0 MWh. Net load 51,508 MW (Enverus) / near-peak. Solar collapse to 7,515 MW (Yes). Houston import constraint HIGH. All 100 MW committed to energy — any AS forfeiture at this price level is suboptimal. Memorial Day load risk: if actual load 2-5% lower than forecast, $59.67 may not clear — mitigated by DA commitment (price locked at clearing).

HE21.notes: SoC = 0 MWh after HE20 full discharge. Cannot discharge at $45.29. Opportunity cost: $45.29 x (any remaining SoC). Strategy forfeits HE21 to concentrate discharge at HE19-20 higher prices. Net load 52,767 MW (Yes) = daily peak — opportunity acknowledged but not actionable.

HE22.notes: SoC=0. Wind strengthening (AG2 13,040 MW HE22). Price declining from peak. Idle.

summary.strategy_label: Stack A — Solar Trough Charge (HE08-10) + Dual-Hour Peak Discharge (HE19-20)
summary.da_as_revenue_note: ESTIMATED — no Smartbidder AS data. Non-Spin 50MW x 7h + ECRS 30MW x 7h at seasonal proxy prices. Low confidence.
summary.net_expected_revenue_note: Energy arbitrage $6,386 + AS estimate $1,045. AS component low confidence.
summary.range_basis: Low: Memorial Day load depression reduces HE20 clearing price by $5-10 (energy -$500 to -$1,000) + AS misses. High: RT spike at HE20 that DA clearing captures, or exceptional ECRS/Non-Spin clearing.
data_quality_flags: AS_PRICES_MISSING: All Smartbidder DA/RT AS prices null. AS revenue is estimate only.
DA_RT_PROBABILITY_MISSING: No P(DA>RT) data. Spread direction uncertain for HE19-20.
AS_PLAYBOOK_MISSING: recommend_as_position.py not run. No prior for AS product selection.
STARTING_SOC_UNCONFIRMED: Assuming 0 MWh; prior day ended at 0 per schedule. Operations to confirm.
